summary: Implement sequential full-quality batch export for selected saved parameter sets with templated names, item progress, failure isolation, and cancellation.

decisions.decision: Snapshot the active project and selected parameter sets once, execute one full-quality export operation at a time, save each artifact before advancing, continue after an ordinary item failure, and on cancellation stop the active operation and mark every unstarted item cancelled.
decisions.rationale: Strict serialization prevents engine pressure and makes completion durable per item. A later failure or cancellation cannot erase item one, while the progress state truthfully distinguishes success, failure, and cancellation for every selected set.

decisions.decision: Default to `{model}-{set}.{ext}`, sanitize model and set tokens for portable leaf names, require the set placeholder, reject paths, unknown placeholders, duplicates, overlong templates, and format-mismatched output extensions before starting engine work.
decisions.rationale: Parameter-set names are user data and may contain filesystem metacharacters. Preflight validation prevents path traversal, accidental overwrites, ambiguous duplicate destinations, and misleading file extensions.

testEvidence.criterion: AC-15.d must export three selected parameter sets sequentially, retain item one when item two fails, and report each outcome.
testEvidence.test: Run the batch coordinator with deferred item operations, then run the workbench integration against an engine spy and artifact destination.

red.outcome: RED: the batch-project-export module did not exist, so no batch tests could load.

green.outcome: GREEN: the coordinator proved sequential start, item-one durability, failure continuation, and cancellation; the workbench integration saved three correctly named full-quality STL artifacts with the three distinct parameter maps.

testEvidence.criterion: The export dialog must select any subset, show the filename template, and expose per-item progress and failures.
testEvidence.test: Exercise the dialog's batch mode with three saved sets, exclude the middle set, and inspect the final two-item progress list.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/files/ProjectExportDialog.test.tsx -t "selects a subset"
red.outcome: RED: no Batch parameter sets control existed. The first implementation then exposed a pooled-event bug when reading checkbox state inside a deferred state updater.

green.outcome: GREEN: 5 files and 22 tests passed, covering subset selection, per-item labels, template routing, output filename validation, single-export regressions, and Files panel integration. TypeScript then passed with no errors.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
